heading: M10+ Clusters
body:
You can pause `M10` or larger clusters:* If they do not use [NVMe storage volumes](nvme-storage).

* For up to 30 days. If you don't resume the cluster within 30
  days, Atlas resumes the cluster.

  > **Note:** You must allow a recently [resume](resume-cluster)
  > cluster to run for at least 60 minutes before pausing the
  > cluster again. This allows the cluster to process all
  > the maintenance jobs that were queued while it was paused.Atlas only charges paused clusters for storage. Atlas does
not charge for any other services or data transfer on paused
clusters.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo pause a running Atlas cluster using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas clusters pause <clusterName> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas clusters pause](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-pause).**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo pause a cluster with the Atlas Administration API, see
[Modify One Cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster).**Ui:**Atlas UITo pause an Atlas cluster using the Atlas UI:* For the cluster you want to pause, click .

* Click Pause Cluster.

* Click Pause Cluster to confirm your choice.
